paragraphs.body: The languages I speak take various forms: gestures, actions, beliefs, the way I do things, and the sayings my parents often repeat. The impact of these different types of language on my personality has been significant.

annotations.comment: The prompt asks you to use an identifiable intro and conclusion form that is effective for your essay's audience and purpose. Which form are you using here?
annotations.context_left: The languages I speak take various forms: gestures, actions, beliefs, the way I do

paragraphs.body: People come from all sorts of different cultures, which can vary greatly. I come from a very diverse culture where knowledge is key. My parents have focused on me and my siblings for almost our entire lives, just for our sake, so that we can live a good life. I remember back when I was a kid; I didn’t understand why my parents were so strict about me wasting my time. I used to play video games all morning while my parents were at work, and as soon as I heard a knock on the door, I would run to my room and pretend I had been studying. Sometimes my mom would genuinely believe I had been doing that, but honestly, it made me feel very guilty. “I knew I shouldn’t have that”, were the thoughts which came to my mind. Nonetheless, I would repeat the same practice on and on for the whole next week. On the other hand, I would also love to get good grades on my exams but that wasn’t happening either. I knew where the problem was, but never tried to work on it.

annotations.text: I come from a very diverse culture where knowledge is key.
annotations.comment: As I read through this essay multiple times, I see lots of places where you're noticing the influence of your family/parents on your values. Here's one of those places. "Knowledge is key."  Another one is: hard work and not wasting time. Another one is: dealing with highs and lows without losing focus...we have a lot to talk about and focus on!

annotations.text: I used to play video games all morning while my parents were at work, and as soon as I heard a knock on the door, I would run to my room and pretend I had been studying.
annotations.comment: Could you begin your essay with a narrative about this moment, perhaps?

paragraphs.body: About two years ago, when I used to spend hours playing video games, I often heard the phrase “Stop wasting your time.” I would think to myself, how could I be wasting my time when I was just enjoying an hour or two with friends? The answer was clear: I wasn’t just wasting my time; I was also wasting my energy. That’s when I decided to join my high school tennis team, which turned out to be much more enjoyable than sitting on the couch staring at a screen for hours. Whenever I felt lazy and didn’t want to go outside to play tennis, my parents would encourage me to get out and be active. There was a time when I was told not to waste my time, and now there was a time when I was pushed out of the house to burn off some energy. This helped me realize that what my parents wanted for me was ultimately good, allowing me to lead a healthier life both physically and mentally. For some, playing video games is perfectly fine, and I respect that; however, my parents wanted more for me because they saw potential in me. Even today, if I spend just five minutes playing a game on my phone, my mom will repeat that same phrase from two years ago. Today, I’m in college, and whenever I earn good grades, my parents are super proud of me. However, when the opposite happens, they remind me that it’s not the end of the journey and encourage me to work hard. In this world, grades ultimately matter for securing a good profession. Working hard is key; if you don’t, you risk falling into a hole. “As you sow, so shall you reap.” Whatever you do in the present will determine the results you see in the future, reflecting your efforts from the past. I remember when I was in 7th grade and participated in a debate competition at our school. I worked hard day and night, with my mom actively supporting me. When the day arrived and I stepped onto the stage, I found myself facing several thousand eyes watching my nervous face. The debate went as planned, and I won the Best Debater award. My parents often said, “Where there is a will, there is a way,” and that mindset helped me achieve this award. When you work hard for something and have a strong will, you will always be rewarded, and that’s how it was for me. I was able to win the same award the following year. Meanwhile, some of my friends wasted their precious time and came to class unprepared. Once, I asked one of them what their future plans were, and they responded as if they didn’t really care about their futures. Many of them said they would continue with their fathers’ businesses, while others were not sure. I feel that carrying on your father’s business is like doing work that has already been done. It does not count as hard work as the hard work has already been done by your father.

annotations.text: Working hard is key; if you don’t, you risk falling into a hole. “As you sow, so shall you reap.” Whatever you do in the present will determine the results you see in the future, reflecting your efforts from the past.
annotations.comment: You're taking a stand, here. Is this idea at the heart of your essay? Let's talk :)

paragraphs.body: Sometimes, when you put in a tremendous amount of effort but don’t achieve the desired result, it can be demotivating. My parents often reminded me, “Sometimes it’s not the right time to be rewarded; perhaps a better reward is on its way.” There have been moments in my life when I fell short of my expectations. For instance, when I was learning tennis for the first time, I practiced tirelessly day and night, hoping to make the school’s team. When tryout day arrived, I felt confident I would succeed, but that wasn’t the outcome. It felt as though all the hours I spent practicing under the scorching sun had been in vain. “I couldn’t make it onto the team,” I told my parents, sadness evident on my face. They lifted my spirits by reminding me that this isn’t the end of the journey: “You have done it before, and you can do it again.” I am truly grateful for such supportive parents; without them, I can’t imagine where I would be today. It’s a powerful truth: parents are indeed a tremendous blessing.

annotations.text: “Sometimes it’s not the right time to be rewarded; perhaps a better reward is on its way.”
annotations.comment: Another maxism. I'm also noticing that all of these quotes have something in common: they're all things your parents have taught you. So, clearly, your parents are at the heart of this essay, right?

annotations.text: I am truly grateful for such supportive parents; without them, I can’t imagine where I would be today. It’s a powerful truth: parents are indeed a tremendous blessing.

paragraphs.body: Losing and winning are all part of life. When you win, you feel good, but when the opposite happens, you can fall into depression. All the sayings my parents have shared with me define true human nature. We all desire a comfortable life with no struggles, but let me remind you that without struggle, you can’t achieve anything you want. In other words, life without struggle is life without meaning. Embracing both the highs and lows helps us grow and appreciate the journey we’re on.

annotations.text: In other words, life without struggle is life without meaning.
annotations.comment: This idea is a slightly different idea than the essay began with (the value of hard work and not wasting time, etc.). Let's see if we can discover the friction in this essay that will lead to a center of gravity that will address the "so what" question. As always, we'll discuss your essay's audience and purpose as well :)

paragraphs.body: To live in our house means to Be knowledgeable Not be lazy Wake up early in the morning Don’t waste time And do something productive
